quote_exact: **Insert Paragraph:** SCANNEDMergeddocketsnotices_20260205_2200__TEXTSNAPSHOT.txt l.2: "Key ROA excerpt (as received): Case 2023-0000005907-PP WATSON v PIGORS; Court C14; Jurist MCNEILL; file 12/04/2023; disposed 12/04/2023 granted; close 12/05/2023; petitioner Emily Watson; respondent Andrew Pigors; entries include petition for PPO, ex parte order, returns of service, motions to modify/terminate, multiple petitions/orders to show cause, hearing entries, extension to 12/04/2025; mittimus 02/28/2025; bench warrant entries; and multiple “Notice to Appear” pages referencing custody/parenting time/child support orders and hearings (e.g., 05/24/2024 objection hearing; 07/17/2024 non-jury trial; 11/26/2025 hearing notice; etc.)." Petitioner denies this statement as false, misleading, incomplete, or prejudicially framed. Petitioner denies the PPO/contempt framing to the extent it recasts lawful or parenting-related communication as harassment/stalking without reliable proof. Petitioner affirms many allegations were narrative escalations tied to requests to see his son. Object to conclusory labels and accusation-driven findings absent competent, authenticated evidence (MRE 401-403, 602, 801-802, 901). Preserve hearing-right and due-process objections in PPO enforcement/modification contexts under MCR 3.707.

Object if the order relies on hearsay, labels, or incomplete evidence rather than admissible proof."

Plaintiff denies threatening conduct, denies unfitness, and denies that communications made to seek contact with his child justify suspension of parenting time. Plaintiff further affirms that repeated incarceration—imposed in proceedings intertwined with parenting-time conflict and PPO enforcement—caused loss of four jobs and two homes, which the Order then repurposes as “mental health concern,” compounding prejudice. Plaintiff affirms the Order conditions the continuation of his objection hearing on a compelled assessment process that is contaminated by disputed narrative inputs and functions as a barrier to being heard, thereby prolonging ex parte deprivation of the parent-child relationship."" Petitioner denies this statement as false, misleading, incomplete, or prejudicially framed. Petitioner denies the PPO/contempt framing to the extent it recasts lawful or parenting-related communication as harassment/stalking without reliable proof. Petitioner affirms many allegations were narrative escalations tied to requests to see his son. quote_exact: > **USER_ASSERTED (sworn-style):** Plaintiff affirms that the Order’s narrative repeatedly converts disputed allegations, colloquial speech, and socioeconomic harm caused by incarceration/litigation into a mental-health insinuation without competent evidentiary findings tied to child safety. Plaintiff denies threatening conduct, denies unfitness, and denies that communications made to seek contact with his child justify suspension of parenting time. Plaintiff further affirms that repeated incarceration—imposed in proceedings intertwined with parenting-time conflict and PPO enforcement—caused loss of four jobs and two homes, which the Order then repurposes as “mental health concern,” compounding prejudice. Plaintiff affirms the Order conditions the continuation of his objection hearing on a compelled assessment process that is contaminated by disputed narrative inputs and functions as a barrier to being heard, thereby prolonging ex parte deprivation of the parent-child relationship.

Plaintiff denies threatening conduct, denies unfitness, and denies that communications made to seek contact with his child justify suspension of parenting time. Plaintiff further affirms that repeated incarceration—imposed in proceedings intertwined with parenting-time conflict and PPO enforcement—caused loss of four jobs and two homes, which the Order then repurposes as “mental health concern,” compounding prejudice. Plaintiff affirms the Order conditions the continuation of his objection hearing on a compelled assessment process that is contaminated by disputed narrative inputs and functions as a barrier to being heard, thereby prolonging ex parte deprivation of the parent-child relationship.

quote_exact: QL-01772,2025-08-22,MEEK2,MEEK2;MEEK3,ppo_or_contempt_allegation,12,bf5f0364-c042-4e93-91cc-d633ca70fdc4.md,bf5f0364-c042-4e93-91cc-d633ca70fdc4.md,,439,md_upload_or_prior_extract,ex parte;incarcerat;mental;mental health;ppo;threaten,"**USER_ASSERTED (sworn-style):** Plaintiff affirms that the Order’s narrative repeatedly converts disputed allegations, colloquial speech, and socioeconomic harm caused by incarceration/litigation into a mental-health insinuation without competent evidentiary findings tied to child safety. Plaintiff further affirms that repeated incarceration—imposed in proceedings intertwined with parenting-time conflict and PPO enforcement—caused loss of four jobs and two homes, which the Order then repurposes as “mental health concern,” compounding prejudice. Plaintiff affirms the Order conditions the continuation of his objection hearing on a compelled assessment process that is contaminated by disputed narrative inputs and functions as a barrier to being heard, thereby prolonging ex parte deprivation of the parent-child relationship.",Petitioner denies the PPO/contempt framing to the extent it recasts lawful or parenting-related communication as harassment/stalking without reliable proof. Plaintiff denies threatening conduct, denies unfitness, and denies that communications made to seek contact with his child justify suspension of parenting time. Plaintiff further affirms that repeated incarceration—imposed in proceedings intertwined with parenting-time conflict and PPO enforcement—caused loss of four jobs and two homes, which the Order then repurposes as “mental health concern,” compounding prejudice. Plaintiff affirms the Order conditions the continuation of his objection hearing on a compelled assessment process that is contaminated by disputed narrative inputs and functions as a barrier to being heard, thereby prolonging ex parte deprivation of the parent-child relationship."" Petitioner denies this statement as false, misleading, incomplete, or prejudicially framed. Petitioner denies the PPO/contempt framing to the extent it recasts lawful or parenting-related communication as harassment/stalking without reliable proof. Preserve hearing-right and due-process objections in PPO enforcement/modification contexts under MCR 3.707."

quote_exact: **Insert Paragraph:** bf5f0364-c042-4e93-91cc-d633ca70fdc4.md l.439: "**USER_ASSERTED (sworn-style):** Plaintiff affirms that the Order’s narrative repeatedly converts disputed allegations, colloquial speech, and socioeconomic harm caused by incarceration/litigation into a mental-health insinuation without competent evidentiary findings tied to child safety. Plaintiff affirms the Order conditions the continuation of his objection hearing on a compelled assessment process that is contaminated by disputed narrative inputs and functions as a barrier to being heard, thereby prolonging ex parte deprivation of the parent-child relationship." Petitioner denies this statement as false, misleading, incomplete, or prejudicially framed. Petitioner denies the PPO/contempt framing to the extent it recasts lawful or parenting-related communication as harassment/stalking without reliable proof. Object to conclusory labels and accusation-driven findings absent competent, authenticated evidence (MRE 401-403, 602, 801-802, 901). Preserve hearing-right and due-process objections in PPO enforcement/modification contexts under MCR 3.707.

quote_exact: QL-01772,2025-08-22,MEEK2,MEEK2;MEEK3,ppo_or_contempt_allegation,12,bf5f0364-c042-4e93-91cc-d633ca70fdc4.md,bf5f0364-c042-4e93-91cc-d633ca70fdc4.md,,439,md_upload_or_prior_extract,ex parte;incarcerat;mental;mental health;ppo;threaten,"**USER_ASSERTED (sworn-style):** Plaintiff affirms that the Order’s narrative repeatedly converts disputed allegations, colloquial speech, and socioeconomic harm caused by incarceration/litigation into a mental-health insinuation without competent evidentiary findings tied to child safety. Plaintiff denies threatening conduct, denies unfitness, and denies that communications made to seek contact with his child justify suspension of parenting time. Plaintiff further affirms that repeated incarceration—imposed in proceedings intertwined with parenting-time conflict and PPO enforcement—caused loss of four jobs and two homes, which the Order then repurposes as “mental health concern,” compounding prejudice. quote_exact: > **USER_ASSERTED (sworn-style):** Plaintiff affirms that the Order’s narrative repeatedly converts disputed allegations, colloquial speech, and socioeconomic harm caused by incarceration/litigation into a mental-health insinuation without competent evidentiary findings tied to child safety. Plaintiff denies threatening conduct, denies unfitness, and denies that communications made to seek contact with his child justify suspension of parenting time. Plaintiff further affirms that repeated incarceration—imposed in proceedings intertwined with parenting-time conflict and PPO enforcement—caused loss of four jobs and two homes, which the Order then repurposes as “mental health concern,” compounding prejudice. Plaintiff affirms the Order conditions the continuation of his objection hearing on a compelled assessment process that is contaminated by disputed narrative inputs and functions as a barrier to being heard, thereby prolonging ex parte deprivation of the parent-child relationship.

quote_exact: > **USER_ASSERTED (sworn-style):** Plaintiff affirms that the Order’s narrative repeatedly converts disputed allegations, colloquial speech, and socioeconomic harm caused by incarceration/litigation into a mental-health insinuation without competent evidentiary findings tied to child safety. Plaintiff denies threatening conduct, denies unfitness, and denies that communications made to seek contact with his child justify suspension of parenting time. Plaintiff further affirms that repeated incarceration—imposed in proceedings intertwined with parenting-time conflict and PPO enforcement—caused loss of four jobs and two homes, which the Order then repurposes as “mental health concern,” compounding prejudice. Plaintiff affirms the Order conditions the continuation of his objection hearing on a compelled assessment process that is contaminated by disputed narrative inputs and functions as a barrier to being heard, thereby prolonging ex parte deprivation of the parent-child relationship.
